title: brachychiton grandiflorus
intro: Brachychiton grandiflorus, also known as the Illawarra flame tree, is a flowering plant native to the eastern coast of Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The Illawarra flame tree is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antidote to snake bites. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
